content: {"cards": [{"name": "Steadfast Guardian", "mana_cost": "{2}{W}", "cmc": 3, "colors": ["W"], "color_identity": ["W"], "type_line": "Creature \u2014 Human Soldier", "oracle_text": "", "flavor_text": "\"In times of peace, I tend the gardens. In times of war, I tend the wounded.\"", "power": "2", "toughness": "4", "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "A vanilla creature that tests restraint in design. At 3 mana, a 2/4 body provides solid defensive stats without any abilities. The high toughness reflects white's defensive nature and ability to protect. Stats total 6 for 3 mana, which is fair but not pushed. Vanilla creatures need to have clean, memorable stats that feel right for their cost."}, {"name": "Skyward Sentinel", "mana_cost": "{1}{W}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["W"], "color_identity": ["W"], "type_line": "Creature \u2014 Bird Soldier", "oracle_text": "Flying", "flavor_text": "The fortress walls extend beyond the stone.", "power": "1", "toughness": "2", "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "A simple keyword-only creature featuring flying, white's secondary evergreen keyword. A 1/2 flyer for 2 mana is a fair common rate - not as aggressive as blue's flyers but provides utility. Flying fits white's theme of small aerial creatures that can get past ground defenses. Bird Soldier typing combines the natural flying creature with white's military organization."}, {"name": "Divine Judgment", "mana_cost": "{2}{W}", "cmc": 3, "colors": ["W"], "color_identity": ["W"], "type_line": "Instant", "oracle_text": "Exile target creature with power 3 or greater.", "flavor_text": "\"The guilty are marked by their own excess.\"", "power": null, "toughness": null, "loyalty": null, "rarity": "uncommon", "design_notes": "White's primary removal at uncommon. Exile is stronger than destroy since it prevents recursion, but the power restriction keeps it from being oppressive. The 'power 3 or greater' condition lets small utility creatures survive while handling major threats. At 3 mana, this is fairly costed for conditional but permanent removal. Demonstrates white's philosophy of judging and removing the most dangerous threats."}, {"name": "Scholarly Insight", "mana_cost": "{1}{U}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["U"], "color_identity": ["U"], "type_line": "Instant", "oracle_text": "Draw a card. Scry 1.", "flavor_text": "\"Knowledge is the compass that guides us through uncertainty.\"", "power": null, "toughness": null, "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "A classic cantrip that tests proper draw templating. 'Draw a card' comes first as the primary effect, followed by scry 1 as the minor added value. At 2 mana, drawing a card plus scry 1 is fair - you pay 1 mana for the card and 1 for the selection. This represents blue's mastery over information and card advantage. The combination teaches players about card selection and deck manipulation."}, {"name": "Dispel Thoughts", "mana_cost": "{1}{U}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["U"], "color_identity": ["U"], "type_line": "Instant", "oracle_text": "Counter target noncreature spell with mana value 3 or less.", "flavor_text": "\"Your grand designs crumble before careful study.\"", "power": null, "toughness": null, "loyalty": null, "rarity": "uncommon", "design_notes": "A counterspell variant that tests stack interaction templating. 'Counter target...' is the standard format. The restriction to noncreature spells makes it specialized against instants, sorceries, and permanents, while the mana value 3 limit keeps it from being too broad. At 2 mana, this is efficiently costed but with meaningful restrictions. Teaches players about the stack and blue's role as the control color."}]}
